era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt (Dynasty VI) continues under Pepi II Neferkare. The Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin is at or near its height. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase. This year falls within the broader Bronze Age, characterized by established trade networks, urban centers, and centralized states across Eurasia.

events.title: Pepi II Neferkare continues reign in Egypt
events.description: Pepi II Neferkare's reign continues during this year. No specific events are attested for 2271 BCE in particular. The broader context is one of continued centralized pharaonic authority, though provincial governors (nomarchs) were gradually accumulating power. Building programs continued, including mortuary temples and administrative structures. The reign is characterized by relative stability, though the seeds of the Old Kingdom's eventual collapse were being sown.

sources.name: Turin King List and Abydos King List
Archaeological evidence from pyramid complexes and administrative records

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for 2271 BCE. The year falls within Pepi II's attested reign, but no contemporary sources record events specific to this year.

events.title: Naram-Sin's Akkadian Empire continues imperial expansion
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues during this period. No specific events are documented for 2271 BCE in particular. The broader context is one of continued Akkadian military campaigns and consolidation of imperial control. The deification of Naram-Sin as a living god had already occurred (or would occur shortly), marking a radical shift in Mesopotamian political theology.

sources.name: Akkadian royal inscriptions and administrative texts

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for 2271 BCE. The year falls within Naram-Sin's attested reign, but no contemporary sources record events specific to this year.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the broader chronological framework for this year is contested, as noted in the -2270 entry.
historiographic_note: 2271 BCE is a year for which no specific events are documented in surviving sources. The historical record for this year is essentially null; what is known is only the broader context of the reigns of Pepi II and Naram-Sin. This is typical for ancient years: only major events (military campaigns, religious innovations, natural disasters, or deaths of rulers) were recorded. The absence of documentation for a specific year does not mean nothing happened—only that nothing of perceived historical significance was recorded or survived.

graph_edges.note: 2271 BCE is one year before 2270 BCE, both within Pepi II's reign.
